_comment: Vehicles this build of FuseOBD can talk to. Uploaded to Fuse-Web's /api/v1/compat/manifest endpoint by tools/upload_compatibility.py at release time. The website's homepage VIN-checker and the My Vehicles 'Check' button look up against the resulting compatible_vehicles table.
_format: Each entry: { year (int|null = any year), make (required), model (string|null = any model), trim (string|null = any trim), coverage ('full'|'partial'|'dtc-only'|'pats-only'|'asbuilt-only'), notes (free text) }

_coverage_legend.full: Module scan, DTC read/clear, live PIDs, PATS, As-Built read/write, Security Access, bus monitor all work
_coverage_legend.partial: OBD-II generic (Mode $01 PIDs, generic DTCs, VIN read) plus best-effort Ford-specific modules

entries.notes: OBD-II generic across all years (1996+). Ford-specific modules (PATS, As-Built, module scanner, PIDs from PCM/BCM/IPC/ABS/RCM/EPAS/APIM/etc) work best on 2008+ CAN-based vehicles. Earlier (1996-2007) ISO9141/J1850 vehicles get generic DTC + VIN + a subset of standard PIDs.
Lincoln shares Ford's module architecture and uses the same UDS/CAN protocols — coverage matches Ford by year. Includes legacy Mark/Town Car/Continental as well as modern Aviator/Navigator/Nautilus.

Mercury shared Ford's module architecture until the brand was discontinued in 2010 — Sable, Grand Marquis, Mariner, Mountaineer, Milan, etc. all supported with the same coverage as the equivalent Ford model-year.
